quiz.explanation: Alive means living; not dead.
Bone means a hard part inside the body.
Bother means to disturb or worry someone.

opts: leader of a team or ship
quiz.explanation: Captain means the leader of a team or ship.
Conclusion means the final decision or ending.
Doubt means a feeling of uncertainty or disbelief.
Explore means to travel through and discover new places.
Foreign means from or relating to another country.
Glad means feeling pleased or happy.
However is used to introduce a contrasting point.
Injustice means unfair treatment or lack of justice.
International means involving more than one country.
Lawyer means a person who practices law.
Mention means to refer to something briefly.
Policy means a set of rules or plans made by an authority.
Social means relating to society and people living together.
Speech means a formal talk given to an audience.
Staff means the group of people who work somewhere.
Toward means in the direction of.
Wood means the hard material from trees.

story.title: The Captain's Speech
story.body: In a small but lively town, there lived a captain named Farhan who was known for being social and kind. He worked with an international organization that fought against injustice in foreign countries. One day, the staff of his office asked him to give a speech at the town hall. Though he had some doubt about his words, Farhan agreed without letting it bother him. He walked toward the wooden stage, built from fine local wood, and began to speak. He mentioned the struggles of people who were barely alive in war zones, who had broken bones and no food. He spoke about a lawyer who had fought a case and reached a conclusion that changed the policy of an entire county. However, the most powerful part was when Farhan said, 'I am glad to live in a world where people still explore ways to fix injustice.' The audience fell silent, then rose in applause. It was an international moment — a reminder that social change begins with one honest voice.

comprehension.q: What did the staff ask Farhan to do?
What was the stage made of?
What did Farhan say he was glad about?
opts: people exploring ways to fix injustice

listening.title: After the Big Speech
listening.script:
A: Did you hear the captain's speech?
B: Yes! I am so glad I attended.
A: Did he mention the injustice in foreign countries?
B: He did. There was no doubt it was powerful.
A: The staff organized it well.
B: However, I think the policy part was the most important.
A: A lawyer also spoke, right?
B: Yes, she talked about reaching a conclusion in a social case.
A: Were many people alive to those ideas?
B: Totally. Everyone walked toward a new understanding.
A: Will this change anything internationally?
B: It might. The captain said exploring solutions is the first bone of progress.
A: I hope so. Don't bother with the doubters.
B: Agreed. Let's move toward change.

comprehension.q: What did B think was most important?
Who else spoke at the event?

speaking.prompt: Talk about a time when you or someone you know stood up against injustice. Use words like doubt, however, glad, policy, and conclusion.
speaking.example: Once I saw an injustice at school. However, I had doubt about speaking up. But I am glad I did. I talked to the staff and we reached a conclusion. The school changed its policy after that.
tips: Say how you felt using 'glad' or 'doubt'
End with a conclusion or result

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: Do you think injustice still exists in our society?

steps.speaker: user
steps.prompt: Give your opinion with a reason.

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: Have you ever seen a foreign policy that seemed unfair?

steps.speaker: app
steps.text: What is your conclusion — can people explore solutions together?

steps.speaker: user
steps.prompt: End with your final thought using 'however' or 'glad'.
